title: Speculative Decoding: Performance or Illusion?
summary_zh: 首篇在生產級推理引擎 (vLLM) 上系統性研究投機解碼的論文。評估五種 SD 變體（n-gram、EAGLE、EAGLE-3、Draft-Model、MTP），發現驗證佔 42–95% 執行時間為主要瓶頸，接受長度在位置和資料集之間波動劇烈。Oracle 分析揭示固定策略與理論上界之間存在顯著差距，自適應方法組合可進一步提升 2.2×。
summary_en: First systematic study of speculative decoding on a production-grade inference engine (vLLM). Evaluates five SD variants (n-gram, EAGLE, EAGLE-3, Draft-Model, MTP) and finds that verification accounts for 42–95% of execution time as the dominant bottleneck, while acceptance length varies wildly across positions and datasets. Oracle analysis reveals substantial gaps between fixed strategies and theoretical upper bounds, with adaptive method combination yielding up to 2.2× further speedup.
